summary: AI Route Safety Engine - ALL TESTS PASSED. Backend: 13/13 tests PASSED (auth, 3-route response, labels, risk scores, geometry, segments, coordinate validation, performance). Frontend: All interactive features working - sidebar nav, Set Route button, map click mode indicator, start/end markers, route polylines, route cards panel, route selection, Reset button, route legend, Selected Route Summary.

test_categories.route_response_structure: 3 PASS - returns routes array, exactly 3 routes, all required fields present
test_categories.route_labels: 2 PASS - includes safest/shortest/balanced labels, recommendation points to safest
test_categories.route_data_validation: 3 PASS - risk scores 0-10, geometry is coordinate array, segments have risk info

frontend.sidebar_navigation: PASS - data-testid='op-nav-route-safety' visible and navigates correctly
frontend.set_route_button: PASS - data-testid='set-route-btn' visible on initial load
frontend.click_mode_indicator: PASS - data-testid='click-mode-indicator' appears after clicking Set Route
frontend.map_container: PASS - data-testid='route-map-container' with Leaflet map centered on Bangalore
frontend.map_click_interaction: PASS - clicking sets green (A) start marker, second click sets red (B) end marker
frontend.route_polylines: PASS - multiple route lines displayed on map with different colors
frontend.route_card_info: PASS - cards show distance (km), duration (min), risk score, danger count
frontend.recommended_badge: PASS - Safest Route card has 'Recommended' badge
frontend.route_selection: PASS - clicking route card updates Selected Route Summary and highlights polyline
frontend.route_legend: PASS - data-testid='route-legend' shows Safest, Balanced, Shortest, Danger point
frontend.reset_button: PASS - data-testid='reset-route-btn' clears all points, routes, and shows empty state
frontend.empty_state: PASS - data-testid='route-empty-state' shows 'Set a route to begin'

route_safety_page.sidebar_highlight: Route Safety nav item highlighted when active
route_safety_page.flow: Set Route → Click start → Click end → View 3 routes → Select route → Reset

critical_code_review_comments: OSRM public routing server (router.project-osrm.org) used for route fetching - no API key needed
If OSRM returns <3 routes, synthetic variants created by offsetting coordinates
Segment scoring uses PostGIS spatial queries against location_risk_zones, location_incidents, device_locations
Sample interval is 500m along route geometry
Cache TTL is 15 minutes per route + hour combination
Route risk calculated as weighted sum: zone(35%) + incident(30%) + time(20%) + isolation(15%)
Geometry is [lng, lat] from backend, frontend converts to [lat, lng] for Leaflet
Selected route highlighted with solid line, others shown as dashed gray
seed_data_creation: None - uses OSRM public routing service and existing PostGIS data
context_for_next_testing_agent: Route Safety Engine complete. Backend uses route_safety_engine.py with OSRM integration for routing and PostGIS for risk scoring. Frontend RouteSafetyIntelligence.jsx has map click interaction for setting start/end points, displays 3 route options with risk visualization. All data-testid attributes properly implemented for testing.
